## What's new

This is was larger update focused on making the editor more beginner-friendly, as well as improving the editing workflow.

- Added a note preview area based on the game, to further help you with visualizing how notes will look. (You can toggle this in the editor)
- Modifiers can now be changed freely, instead of having to select them before placing a note.
- Additionally, you can now select and right-click notes to remove specific modifiers from them as well.
- Added labels to the columns to make it more beginner-friendly, as well as a popup when you haven't set a timing point yet.
- Added a keybind section for general editor shortcuts (this does not include notes and modifiers, which are still bound to 1-9 and q-p)
- Added a help button at the top of the editor, to let users know of the general workflow when creating a map.
- Improved export support for Linux, and added a general tip for getting charts into the game.
- Fixed a bug where all notes played the same sample, even with different modifiers. (Only if no custom hitsound is set)
- Removed the Websocket mod feature, there are better ways to test maps quickly now.

**Happy mapping!**
